direction.description: Proxy URI/host support must not allow unbounded input to reach fixed-size resolver storage.

spec_ref.quote_or_summary: CoAP proxying can use URI-derived host information.

statement: Proxy hostnames accepted from CoAP requests must be length-checked or rejected before copying into fixed-size resolver storage.
condition: A CoAP proxy-enabled server handles attacker-controlled Proxy-Uri or host data.
expected_behavior: The implementation bounds-checks the hostname and rejects or safely truncates only where truncation cannot alter authority semantics.
violation_pattern: A hostname derived from request data is copied into a fixed stack buffer without length checks.
bound_goal: Safe proxy request handling and authority/host parsing

review.review_notes: May be downgraded or rejected if CVE review shows generic API misuse rather than CoAP option semantics.
